events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes a VFD installation in service with a missing load reactor on long motor leads. This is a known issue: when VFDs drive motors over long cable runs, the high-frequency PWM output causes voltage reflections/doubling at the motor terminals, leading to insulation breakdown and premature motor failure. Load reactors or dV/dt filters are required to protect motor insulation.

This is a non-compliant condition that will lead to motor degradation or failure. The missing load reactor on long motor leads is a design/installation deficiency that puts the motor at risk of insulation failure due to reflected wave phenomena.

Decision: fail (non-compliant, dangerous to motor asset)
Risk: high
Work stage: in-service
Component conditions: degraded (motor insulation likely degrading), non-compliant (missing required reactor)
Percent complete: 100 (in service)
Confidence: high, maybe 0.85

Let me write the JSON.

    "Perform insulation resistance (Megger) test on motor windings to assess insulation damage before re-energizing",
    "Recommission with reactor installed and verify motor terminal voltage peak levels are within insulation limits"
  ],
  "rationale": "VFD PWM switching on long motor leads causes reflected-wave phenomena that can raise motor terminal peak voltages to ~2× DC bus voltage, rapidly degrading winding insulation. Manufacturer manuals specify load reactors or dV/dt filters above certain cable lengths; their omission is a non-compliant installation defect that places the motor in a degraded/high-risk condition while in service.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 430 (Article 430 motor circuits, overcurrent/overload protection context)",
    "VFD manufacturer installation manual (output reactor/dV/dt filter requirements for long motor leads)"
  ]
